paper_title: Constraints to Sustained and Inclusive Growth in Sri Lanka: An Initial Diagnosis and Key Topics for Discussion
research_answer: Yes — retreaded tires are a notable exception to the pattern seen in Sri Lanka's other major exports. Sri Lanka holds a remarkable 33% global share of retreaded tire exports and competes against much wealthier nations: Belgium ($46,000 GDP per capita), Germany ($37,000), Japan ($35,000), and Brazil ($11,000). This is the opposite of the situation in garments and tea, where Sri Lanka competes against lower-income countries. Retreaded tires account for about 3% of Sri Lanka's exports, suggesting this is a niche success story that bucks the general trend but hasn't been scaled into broader export diversification.
supporting_quotes: Retreaded Tires: Middle and high income competitors... Global Share of Retreaded Tires Exports (Net) in 2014 [showing Sri Lanka 33%, China 14%, Japan 11%, Brazil 9%] with GDP per capita showing Sri Lanka at ~$4,000 vs Belgium $47,000, Germany $37,000, Japan $36,000, Brazil $11,000
